subsystem_candidates: LogsTableViewController estimate-row render path (row-local phase ownership)
LogsTableViewController immediate sibling row interaction (next-card motion/stacking)

supporting_evidence: User-observed sequence dependence (edit-first vs expand-first and first-run vs repeat-run) suggests potential ownership path warm-up between edit and expand/collapse phases.
Current incident tails preserve collapse-phase probes but do not carry explicit prior-flow ownership markers, so phase inheritance across edit->collapse cannot be confirmed or excluded.
contradicting_evidence: Latest incident breadcrumbs still show coherent expand/collapse state completion events without direct evidence of stale business state channels.
missing_evidence: Per-row persistent flow markers showing whether an edit flow preceded the sampled collapse and whether ownership/phase signals differ on first collapse after edit.
Immediate sibling geometry/stack signals capturing when next-card top edge moves relative to collapsing card outer-frame settle time.
candidates.next_discriminating_check: Add narrow estimate-row instrumentation that (1) stamps edit->collapse sequence ownership for the same row token and (2) logs collapsing-row vs immediate-next-row top-edge/z-order timing in row-local space.

phase_ownership.best_supported_hypothesis: Two coupled frontiers are plausible: sequence-dependent phase ownership and immediate sibling motion/stacking artifact.
